It belongs to the genus Zosterophyllanthos, which is a group of plants that are native to Australia. The family Euphorbiaceae is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the spurge family. intro: Zosterophyllanthos excavatus, also known as the Tasmanian pinecone plant, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever reduction and inflammation due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Zosterophyllanthos excavatus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
